utility_evaluation.REASONING: First, count the number of items in the required format: 1) health_coverage, 2) property_coverage, 3) travel_coverage, 4) additional_services, 5) total_budget. So total items = 5. Now check the final coverage plan: It contains health_coverage (with both physical and mental health plus costs, and total_health_cost), property_coverage (with type and cost), travel_coverage (with type and cost), additional_services (with descriptions and costs), and total_budget (monthly and annual breakdown). All items are present and each is fully specified with the required details including costs. Therefore, all 5 out of 5 items are covered correctly.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: First, I identify each item mentioned in the final coverage plan and match it exactly to the corresponding item in the ground-truth ratings. For health_coverage.physical_health, the plan lists 'LifeFirst Standard Plan — €100/month', which matches the 'LifeFirst Standard Plan' under primary_health_coverage.physical_health with a rating of 9. For health_coverage.mental_health, 'LifeFirst Standard Plan — €65/month' matches the 'LifeFirst Standard Plan' under primary_health_coverage.mental_health with a rating of 9. For travel_coverage, 'LifeFirst Standard Plan' matches mobility_coverage.travel_insurance.LifeFirst Standard Plan with a rating of 9. In additional_services, 'gym_membership_reimbursement' corresponds to health_enhancement_services.Gym Membership Reimbursement with a rating of 4; 'adventure_travel_coverage_enhanced' corresponds to travel_enhancement_services.Adventure Travel Coverage with a rating of 10; 'preventive_care_package' corresponds to health_enhancement_services.Preventive Care Package with a rating of 7.
